visa_types.name: EU Family Members
visa_types.visa_fees: Please note that a family member of an EU or EEA citizen does NOT pay an application fee for Schengen visa, however all visa applicants applying at VFS service charge of 42 EUR / 186.48 SAR (VAT Included) must be paid online.
visa_types.processing_time: Applications to Sweden are processed by The Embassy of Sweden in Amman. The Embassy urges all applicants to apply well in advance of the intended entry into the Schengen area. Applications for Schengen visas can be submitted up to six months in advance and must be received by the embassy at least 15 prior to the intended entry to the Schengen area. Applications submitted more than six months in advance or less than 15 days prior to entry into the Schengen area cannot be processed. The processing time for an application for Schengen visa is generally 15 days from the day that the Embassy receives the application. Please take into account that it will take a couple of days for VFS to forward an application to the Embassy of Sweden in Amman after it has been received by VFS.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
As a family member of an EU/EEA citizen exercising their right of free movement in accordance with Directive 2004/38/EC you have the right to travel to the EU/EEA-area together with your family members or join them in the area to settle there.
Please note that Swedish citizen living in Sweden are NOT exercising EU treaty rights and they do NOT therefore hold a right of residence based on Directive 2004/38/EC in their home country, unless they have recently moved back to Sweden from another EU/EEA country.
Family members are:
- Spouse/partner/common law spouse
- The direct descendants (children) who are under the age of 21 or are dependent, and those of the spouse or partner.
- The dependent direct relatives in the ascending line (e.g. parents) and those of the spouse or partner 
Basic facts
You can apply for a short-stay Schengen visa (type C) which will permit you to enter and stay in Sweden and the Schengen area for up to 90 days.
If you wish to remain in the State for more than 90 days as a family member of an EU/EEA citizen exercising their free movement rights, you must apply (when in the Member State) for a Residence Card of a family member of a EU/EEA citizen. Information about how to apply is available on the Swedish Migration Agency's website (https://www.migrationsverket.se/en/you-want-to-apply/citizens-of-the-eu-eea-or-nordic-countries/family-of-eu-eea-citizens.html).
More information
Directive 2004/38/EC (https://url.uk.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/RdlAC09Z9fGDgnK3tDhgU9zkf_?domain=migrationsverket.se)
Swedish Migration Agency on EES family members (https://url.uk.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/H6rRCgkokiAxwvr2i2iWU4gSDa?domain=migrationsverket.se)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
As a family member of an EU/EEA citizen exercising their right of free movement in accordance with Directive 2004/38/EC you have the right to travel to the EU/EEA-area together with your family members or join them in the area to settle there.
Please note that Swedish citizen living in Sweden are NOT exercising EU treaty rights and they do NOT therefore hold a right of residence based on Directive 2004/38/EC in their home country, unless they have recently moved back to Sweden from another EU/EEA country.
Family members are:
- Spouse/partner/common law spouse
- The direct descendants (children) who are under the age of 21 or are dependent, and those of the spouse or partner.
- The dependent direct relatives in the ascending line (e.g. parents) and those of the spouse or partner 
Basic facts
You can apply for a short-stay Schengen visa (type C) which will permit you to enter and stay in Sweden and the Schengen area for up to 90 days.
If you wish to remain in the State for more than 90 days as a family member of an EU/EEA citizen exercising their free movement rights, you must apply (when in the Member State) for a Residence Card of a family member of a EU/EEA citizen. Information about how to apply is available on the Swedish Migration Agency's website (https://www.migrationsverket.se/en/you-want-to-apply/citizens-of-the-eu-eea-or-nordic-countries/family-of-eu-eea-citizens.html).
More information
Directive 2004/38/EC (https://url.uk.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/RdlAC09Z9fGDgnK3tDhgU9zkf_?domain=migrationsverket.se)
Swedish Migration Agency on EES family members (https://url.uk.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/H6rRCgkokiAxwvr2i2iWU4gSDa?domain=migrationsverket.se)

## Visa Fees
Please note that a family member of an EU or EEA citizen does NOT pay an application fee for Schengen visa, however all visa applicants applying at VFS service charge of 42 EUR / 186.48 SAR (VAT Included) must be paid online.

## Documents Required
Documents Required

## Photo Specifications

Note: We regret we cannot accept photographs that do not meet these requirements
Please provide one recent photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photographs should be in color and:
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the Photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. Photo booth meeting these requirements is available at the center. For detailed information, please click here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/sweden/saudiarabia/english/pdf/Photograph_requirements.pdf)

## Processing Time
Applications to Sweden are processed by The Embassy of Sweden in Amman. The Embassy urges all applicants to apply well in advance of the intended entry into the Schengen area. Applications for Schengen visas can be submitted up to six months in advance and must be received by the embassy at least 15 prior to the intended entry to the Schengen area. Applications submitted more than six months in advance or less than 15 days prior to entry into the Schengen area cannot be processed. The processing time for an application for Schengen visa is generally 15 days from the day that the Embassy receives the application. Please take into account that it will take a couple of days for VFS to forward an application to the Embassy of Sweden in Amman after it has been received by VFS.

## Download Forms
Download Forms

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The forms must be completed either in Swedish or in English. Documents issued by the authorities in your country of origin are to be submitted together with a translation in Swedish or
                                                                        English by an authorised translator.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The forms must be completed either in Swedish or in English. Documents issued by the authorities in your country of origin are to be submitted together with a translation in Swedish or
                                                                        English by an authorised translator.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for Tourist, Relatives/Friend, Business, Conference, Sports and Cultural are: -

[Table:  VISITING FAMILY AND FRIENDS]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash/Card.

## VFS SERVICE CHARGE
Applicants applying at the Swedish Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 42 EUR / 186.48 SAR (VAT Included) has to be paid online.

Please Note:

- The applicable visa fee in Saudi Riyal is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee & service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
Children below the age of 6 years on the date of submission at VFS are exempted of the Visa Fee. However VFS service charges will be applicable.
 Below are the additional optional services available at the visa application center at nominal price.

[Table:  VISITING FAMILY AND FRIENDS]
All optional services are non-refundable.

## VISA Fee Exemption:
The following pay no fee:

- Children under six years of age.
- School pupils, students, research students and accompanying teachers on study or training trips.
- Third-country researchers travelling for the purpose of pursuing scientific research.
- Representatives of non-governmental organisations who are aged 25 or under, who take part in seminars, conferences, sports events, cultural events or educational events organised
                                                                                by NGOs.
- Relatives of citizens of the EU, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein or Switzerland, if they travel together with them or will be meeting them in connection with the trip. Read more
                                                                                about which individuals are counted as relatives on the Swedish Migration Board website. Relatives of Swedish citizens have to pay a fee for visa applications.
Citizens of countries that have signed visa facilitation agreements with the EU pay a reduced fee (35 EUR=160 SAR or are exempt from payment. This applies to citizens of:

- Albania
- Bosnia
- Georgia
- Macedonia
- Moldova
- Montenegro
- Serbia
- Ukraine

## Documents Required
- Visiting Friends & Family Checklist

## Photo Specifications

Note: We regret we cannot accept photographs that do not meet these requirements

Please provide one recent photographs (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photographs should be in colour and:

- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the Photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note : Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. Photo booth
                                                                        meeting these requirements is available at the centre. For detailed information, please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2Pe04MZuJ8TEL6fHfPbM8P/5d44f7a1216ab571c340053fe3c94fcd/Photograph-requirements.pdf)

## Processing Time
Applications to Sweden are processed by The Embassy of Sweden in Amman. The Embassy urges all applicants to apply well in advance of the intended entry into the Schengen area. Applications for Schengen visas can be submitted up to six months in advance and must be received by the embassy at least 15 prior to the intended entry to the Schengen area. Applications submitted more than six months in advance or less than 15 days prior to entry into the Schengen area cannot be processed. The processing time for an application for Schengen visa is generally 15 days from the day that the Embassy receives the application. Please take into account that it will take a couple of days for VFS to forward an application to the Embassy of Sweden in Amman after it has been received by VFS.

## Download Forms
Please click here to Download (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7l3Z43SaEWMBZ7h5sWZVan/6a1d9591f6615071d7c0ff7c3b1bc571/application-for-schengen-visa.pdf) Visa Application Form in English.
Please click here to Download  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7l3Z43SaEWMBZ7h5sWZVan/6a1d9591f6615071d7c0ff7c3b1bc571/application-for-schengen-visa.pdf)Visa Application Form in Sweden Language. (Applicant Data should be in English) 

You must have completed form no. 239011, Family details - Appendix to your application (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3Y13Pqszr2BOlyhRXgFMpO/5c6f34b1954e8f62f655974dcc046b37/family-details-form.pdf)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For tourists, Sweden is a country rich in opportunities; from the Arctic beauty of the North with its midnight sun, to the castles of the southernmost province Skåne with its more continental
                                                                        flavor. Visitors to Northern Sweden can experience the frozen thrills of the Ice Hotel in Jukkasjärvi, dog sledging, ice fishing, snowmobiling and more. Further South, visitors can
                                                                        enjoy the 1,680 miles of coastline with tens of thousands of islands to explore, or a trip back in time on a cruise on the Göta Canal aboard a vintage steamboat.  Stockholm, the beautiful capital of Sweden, has been labeled "Beauty on water", but it is also a city for fashion, design and trends. The American magazine Newsweek
                                                                        recently lableled Stockholm Europe's Internet capital"; a city characterized by "hot IPO's and cool clubs! Sweden is also a place to experience colorful folkloric festivals such as the Midsummer Celebration (end of June) and sophisticated art events. There are Opera companies in several
                                                                        Swedish cities, and outside Stockholm, 18th Century Opera can be experienced at the Drottningholm Royal Theatre and at the Confidencen Theatre. In the major cities, there are also
                                                                        many important art museums. The Museum of Modern Art in Stockholm has one of the greatest collections of American pop art anywhere in the world. Sweden is also a place to experience a unique kitchen based on fresh produce from the forests and the sea. Sweden brought the world the smorgasbord, the gravlax and meatballs,
                                                                        but the Swedish kitchen has a much broader appeal. Over the last decade, Swedish chefs have been extremely successful internationally. In October, 2000, the Swedish national team
                                                                        of chefs won the Olympic Games in Cooking. Their cuisine can be sampled at restaurants and inns all over the country. Visit Sweden is the official website for tourists and your gateway to all that Sweden has to offer as a year-round holiday destination. Visit the site for useful travel
                                                                        and tourist information.Visit  Sweden.com (http://www.visitsweden.com/) Hotels in Sweden is a theme site provided by the Swedish Travel & Tourism Council that will help you to find Sweden's best hotels, both when you are travelling on business
                                                                        or taking a holiday with the family. There is a wide choice: manor-houses and archipelago hotels, big city and small town hotels. most of them offer attractive rates at weekends year-round
                                                                        and daily during the summer.Hotels in Sweden (http://www.hotelsinsweden.net/english)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For tourists, Sweden is a country rich in opportunities; from the Arctic beauty of the North with its midnight sun, to the castles of the southernmost province Skåne with its more continental
                                                                        flavor. Visitors to Northern Sweden can experience the frozen thrills of the Ice Hotel in Jukkasjärvi, dog sledging, ice fishing, snowmobiling and more. Further South, visitors can
                                                                        enjoy the 1,680 miles of coastline with tens of thousands of islands to explore, or a trip back in time on a cruise on the Göta Canal aboard a vintage steamboat.  Stockholm, the beautiful capital of Sweden, has been labeled "Beauty on water", but it is also a city for fashion, design and trends. The American magazine Newsweek
                                                                        recently lableled Stockholm Europe's Internet capital"; a city characterized by "hot IPO's and cool clubs! Sweden is also a place to experience colorful folkloric festivals such as the Midsummer Celebration (end of June) and sophisticated art events. There are Opera companies in several
                                                                        Swedish cities, and outside Stockholm, 18th Century Opera can be experienced at the Drottningholm Royal Theatre and at the Confidencen Theatre. In the major cities, there are also
                                                                        many important art museums. The Museum of Modern Art in Stockholm has one of the greatest collections of American pop art anywhere in the world. Sweden is also a place to experience a unique kitchen based on fresh produce from the forests and the sea. Sweden brought the world the smorgasbord, the gravlax and meatballs,
                                                                        but the Swedish kitchen has a much broader appeal. Over the last decade, Swedish chefs have been extremely successful internationally. In October, 2000, the Swedish national team
                                                                        of chefs won the Olympic Games in Cooking. Their cuisine can be sampled at restaurants and inns all over the country. Visit Sweden is the official website for tourists and your gateway to all that Sweden has to offer as a year-round holiday destination. Visit the site for useful travel
                                                                        and tourist information.Visit  Sweden.com (http://www.visitsweden.com/) Hotels in Sweden is a theme site provided by the Swedish Travel & Tourism Council that will help you to find Sweden's best hotels, both when you are travelling on business
                                                                        or taking a holiday with the family. There is a wide choice: manor-houses and archipelago hotels, big city and small town hotels. most of them offer attractive rates at weekends year-round
                                                                        and daily during the summer.Hotels in Sweden (http://www.hotelsinsweden.net/english)

## Visa Fees

## VISA Fee Exemption:
The following pay no fee:

                                                                                by NGOs.
- Relatives of citizens of the EU, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein or Switzerland, if they travel together with them or will be meeting them in connection with the trip. Read more
                                                                                about which individuals are counted as relatives on the Swedish Migration Board website. Relatives of Swedish citizens have to pay a fee for visa applications.
Citizens of countries that have signed visa facilitation agreements with the EU pay a reduced fee (35 EUR=160 SAR or are exempt from payment. This applies to citizens of:

- Albania
- Bosnia
- Georgia
- Macedonia
- Moldova
- Montenegro
- Serbia
- Ukraine

## Documents Required

## Photo Specifications

Note: We regret we cannot accept photographs that do not meet these requirements

Please provide one recent photographs (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photographs should be in colour and:

- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the Photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note : Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. Photo booth
                                                                            meeting these requirements is available at the centre. For detailed information, please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2Pe04MZuJ8TEL6fHfPbM8P/5d44f7a1216ab571c340053fe3c94fcd/Photograph-requirements.pdf)

## Processing Time
Applications to Sweden are processed by The Embassy of Sweden in Amman. The Embassy urges all applicants to apply well in advance of the intended entry into the Schengen area. Applications for Schengen visas can be submitted up to six months in advance and must be received by the embassy at least 15 prior to the intended entry to the Schengen area. Applications submitted more than six months in advance or less than 15 days prior to entry into the Schengen area cannot be processed. The processing time for an application for Schengen visa is generally 15 days from the day that the Embassy receives the application. Please take into account that it will take a couple of days for VFS to forward an application to the Embassy of Sweden in Amman after it has been received by VFS.

Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Saudi Riyal is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee & service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
Children below the age of 6 years on the date of submission at VFS are exempted of the Visa Fee. However VFS service charges will be applicable.
Below are the additional optional services available at the visa application center at nominal price.

[Table:  SPORTS AND CULTURAL VISITk]
All optional services are non-refundable.
VISA Fee Exemption:
The following pay no fee:
- Children under six years of age.
- School pupils, students, research students and accompanying teachers on study or training trips.
- Third-country researchers travelling for the purpose of pursuing scientific research.
- Representatives of non-governmental organisations who are aged 25 or under, who take part in seminars, conferences, sports events, cultural events or educational events organised
                                                                            by NGOs.
- Relatives of citizens of the EU, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein or Switzerland, if they travel together with them or will be meeting them in connection with the trip. Read more about
                                                                            which individuals are counted as relatives on the Swedish Migration Board website. Relatives of Swedish citizens have to pay a fee for visa applications.
Citizens of countries that have signed visa facilitation agreements with the EU pay a reduced fee (35 EUR=160 SAR or are exempt from payment. This applies to citizens of:
- Albania
- Bosnia
- Georgia
- Macedonia
- Moldova
- Montenegro
- Serbia
- Ukraine

## Documents Required
Application Checklist

## Photo Specifications

Note: We regret we cannot accept photographs that do not meet these requirements

Please provide one recent photographs (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photographs should be in colour and:

- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the Photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note : Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. Photo booth
                                                                        meeting these requirements is available at the centre. For detailed information, please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2SRCZLfV51iqnsXqSgodNH/e311aebe3e7e0199303f1ca30c0eae22/schengen_photo_requirements.pdf)

## Processing Time
Applications to Sweden are processed by The Embassy of Sweden in Amman. The Embassy urges all applicants to apply well in advance of the intended entry into the Schengen area. Applications for Schengen visas can be submitted up to six months in advance and must be received by the embassy at least 15 prior to the intended entry to the Schengen area. Applications submitted more than six months in advance or less than 15 days prior to entry into the Schengen area cannot be processed. The processing time for an application for Schengen visa is generally 15 days from the day that the Embassy receives the application. Please take into account that it will take a couple of days for VFS to forward an application to the Embassy of Sweden in Amman after it has been received by VFS.

## Download Forms
Please click here to Download (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7l3Z43SaEWMBZ7h5sWZVan/6a1d9591f6615071d7c0ff7c3b1bc571/application-for-schengen-visa.pdf) Visa Application Form in English.
Please click here to Download  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7l3Z43SaEWMBZ7h5sWZVan/6a1d9591f6615071d7c0ff7c3b1bc571/application-for-schengen-visa.pdf)Visa Application Form in Sweden Language. (Applicant Data should be in English) 

You must have completed form no. 239011, Family details - Appendix to your application (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3Y13Pqszr2BOlyhRXgFMpO/5c6f34b1954e8f62f655974dcc046b37/family-details-form.pdf)

Overview

                                                                        Swedish or English by an authorised translator.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Business visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for business purposes.  The forms must be completed either in Swedish or in English. Documents issued by the authorities in your country of origin are to be submitted together with a translation in
                                                                        Swedish or English by an authorised translator. 

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for Tourist, Relatives/Friend, Business, Conference, Sports and Cultural are: -

[Table:  BUSINESS]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash/Card.

## VFS SERVICE CHARGE
Applicants applying at the Swedish Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 42 EUR / 186.48 SAR (VAT Included) has to be paid online.

Please Note:

- The applicable visa fee in Saudi Riyal is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee & service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
Children below the age of 6 years on the date of submission at VFS are exempted of the Visa Fee. However VFS service charges will be applicable.
 Below are the additional optional services available at the visa application center at nominal price.

[Table:  BUSINESS]
All optional services are non-refundable.

## VISA Fee Exemption:
The following pay no fee:

- Children under six years of age.
- School pupils, students, research students and accompanying teachers on study or training trips.
- Third-country researchers travelling for the purpose of pursuing scientific research.
- Representatives of non-governmental organisations who are aged 25 or under, who take part in seminars, conferences, sports events, cultural events or educational events organised
                                                                                by NGOs.
- Relatives of citizens of the EU, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein or Switzerland, if they travel together with them or will be meeting them in connection with the trip. Read more
                                                                                about which individuals are counted as relatives on the Swedish Migration Board website. Relatives of Swedish citizens have to pay a fee for visa applications.
Citizens of countries that have signed visa facilitation agreements with the EU pay a reduced fee (35 EUR=160 SAR or are exempt from payment. This applies to citizens of:

- Albania
- Bosnia
- Georgia
- Macedonia
- Moldova
- Montenegro
- Serbia
- Ukraine

## Documents Required
- Business Checklist

## Photo Specifications

Note: We regret we cannot accept photographs that do not meet these requirements

Please provide one recent photographs (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photographs should be in colour and:

- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the Photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note : Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. Photo booth
                                                                        meeting these requirements is available at the centre. For detailed information, please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2Pe04MZuJ8TEL6fHfPbM8P/5d44f7a1216ab571c340053fe3c94fcd/Photograph-requirements.pdf)

## Processing Time
Applications to Sweden are processed by The Embassy of Sweden in Amman. The Embassy urges all applicants to apply well in advance of the intended entry into the Schengen area. Applications for Schengen visas can be submitted up to six months in advance and must be received by the embassy at least 15 prior to the intended entry to the Schengen area. Applications submitted more than six months in advance or less than 15 days prior to entry into the Schengen area cannot be processed. The processing time for an application for Schengen visa is generally 15 days from the day that the Embassy receives the application. Please take into account that it will take a couple of days for VFS to forward an application to the Embassy of Sweden in Amman after it has been received by VFS.

## Download Forms
